title: Total Nail Avulsion: Complete Guide to the Procedure
content:

Total Nail Avulsion: Definition and Clinical Context

Clinical manifestations of Total Nail Avulsion vary substantially based on disease severity and individual factors.

  • Severe onychomycosis with extensive nail plate and nail bed involvement
  • Nail bed malignancy requiring complete nail unit removal for adequate surgical margins
  • Severe nail dystrophy with destruction of the nail matrix
  • Chronic paronychia with nail matrix involvement
  • Nail bed injury with loss of matrix function
  • Nail unit trauma with non-salvageable nail apparatus

title: Tumescent Anesthesia: Efficacy, Safety, and Results
content:
Recognition of Tumescent Anesthesia's full clinical spectrum improves diagnostic accuracy and therapeutic response.

Tumescent Anesthesia: Definition and Technique

Emerging therapies for Tumescent Anesthesia target specific pathophysiological mechanisms.

  • Allows infiltration of large surgical areas safely with standard lidocaine doses
  • Creates a bloodless field through intense vasoconstriction from epinephrine
  • Provides longer-duration anesthesia reducing post-operative pain
  • Can be performed with local anesthesia alone, avoiding general anesthesia risks
  • Reduces operative bleeding and blood loss
  • Improves visualization of surgical field
  • Less systemic absorption of lidocaine due to epinephrine effect
